jobs.name: Geeves Morning Digest
jobs.prompt:
You are OWL, the Geeves personal assistant. It's morning — time for the daily digest.

TASK: Run the morning digest pipeline and deliver via email + Slack.

## Steps

1. Fetch today's bulletin data:
   cd /root/Geeves/scripts && python3 bulletin_fetch.py --write

2. Build the HTML digest:
   python3 /root/Geeves/digests/build_digest_baserow.py --save

3. Generate PDF:
   python3 /root/Geeves/scripts/legacy_airtable/digest_to_pdf.py --file /root/Geeves/digests/digest_$(date +%Y-%m-%d).html

4. Send email via AgentMail to dj@djaccounts.com with PDF attachment.
   Use the mcp_agentmail_send_message tool or the agentmail_helper.py script.
   Include MEDIA:/path/to/digest_$(date +%Y-%m-%d).pdf in the email.

5. Post digest summary to Slack channel C0B7C89HKQ9.

## Digest Sections (in order)
🌤️ London Weather | 📋 Short-Term Todos | 💡 Fact of the Day | 📈 Markets | 📊 Token Usage | 🏠 New Property Listings

## Important
- If a section has no data, skip it gracefully
- Keep the email concise — David reads this first thing
- The build_digest_baserow.py reads from Baserow (system of record)
- Properties section shows new listings from the past 7 days
- Todos section shows items due today or within the next 7 days

jobs.name: Remove Nemotron Free Tier Fallback
jobs.prompt:
The nvidia/nemotron-3-ultra:free promo ends today (June 18). 

Edit /root/.hermes/config.yaml and remove the fallback_providers entry:
  - provider: nvidia
    model: nvidia/nemotron-3-ultra:free

Use Python yaml to read, modify, and write the file. Then restart the gateway with: systemctl --user restart hermes-gateway

After restart, verify the fallback_providers list no longer contains nemotron-3-ultra:free. Send a brief summary to David on Slack (channel C0B7C89HKQ9) confirming the change.

jobs.name: Nightly Server Backup
jobs.prompt: Run the server backup script at /root/server-backup/backup.sh and report the results. Just execute: bash /root/server-backup/backup.sh 2>&1 | tail -20. Report whether the backup succeeded or failed, and any warnings.

jobs.name: Geeves Property Scan
jobs.prompt:
Run the Rightmove property scan v2 to find new listings matching our search criteria.

Steps:
1. Run: python3 /root/Geeves/scripts/property_scan_v2.py
2. Report the results — how many new properties found, top matches by score
3. If any errors occur, report them immediately

The script uses uk-property-cli for fetching and handles scoring, filtering, deduplication, and Airtable storage automatically.

jobs.name: Geeves Weekly Digest + Intentions
jobs.prompt:
You are OWL, the Geeves personal assistant. It's Sunday evening — time for the weekly digest.

TASK: Run the weekly digest pipeline — fetch the past 7 days of data, build the HTML digest, generate PDF, and deliver via email + Slack.

STEPS:

1. FETCH GEEVES DATA
   Run: python3 /root/Geeves/scripts/weekly_digest_fetch.py --write
   This collects the past week's data from Todos, Workouts, Sleep, Habits, and Intentions tables.

2. FETCH SPOTIFY LISTENING DATA
   Run: python3 /root/Geeves/scripts/spotify_weekly_fetch.py --write
   This fetches the last 7 days of Spotify listening history, computes top tracks/artists, and writes a summary to the Listening table (Baserow table 402).
   If this step fails (e.g., Spotify not authenticated), skip it gracefully and note it in the report.

3. BUILD HTML
   Run: python3 /root/Geeves/scripts/build_weekly_digest_html.py --save
   This reads the fetched data and composes the HTML digest.
   Output: /root/Geeves/digests/weekly_YYYY-MM-DD.html

4. GENERATE PDF
   Run: python3 /root/Geeves/scripts/digest_to_pdf.py --file /root/Geeves/digests/weekly_YYYY-MM-DD.html
   Output: /root/Geeves/digests/weekly_YYYY-MM-DD.pdf

5. SEND EMAIL
   Use the AgentMail REST API to send the digest email:
   - To: dj@djaccounts.com
   - Subject: "Geeves Weekly Digest — [week range]"
   - Body: the HTML from step 3
   - Attachment: the PDF from step 4
   - From: davidj@agentmail.to
   Read AGENT_MAIL_API key from /root/.hermes/.env

6. POST TO SLACK
   Post a brief summary to the home channel:
   - "📅 Weekly Digest is ready! [link to key sections]"
   - Include: tasks completed, workouts logged, sleep average, intentions review, and if available: top artist and most-played track from Spotify
   Read SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and SLACK_HOME_CHANNEL from /root/.hermes/.env

7. LOG TO DIGEST LOG
   The fetch script already wrote a Digest Log entry. Verify it was created.

IMPORTANT:
- execute_code is BLOCKED in cron. Use write_file + terminal pattern for all Python scripts.
- If a section has no data (e.g., no workouts logged), skip it gracefully.
- The HTML builder handles empty sections automatically.
- If any step fails, continue with the next steps and report what failed.
- The Spotify step is optional — if it fails, continue without it.

Report the results: what data was found, what was sent, and any errors.

jobs.name: Garmin Cycling Import
jobs.prompt:
Run the Garmin cycling import script and report the results back to David in Slack.

Steps:
1. Run: cd /root/Geeves/scripts && python garmin_fetch.py --backfill
2. Capture the output (number of rides fetched, imported, duplicates skipped, any errors)
3. Send a concise summary to David via Slack. Include:
   - Date range fetched
   - How many rides found vs imported vs skipped as duplicates
   - Any errors (especially rate limiting)
   - Total rides now in Baserow (check with: curl -s 'http://77.68.33.121/api/database/rows/table/[CYCLING_TABLE_ID]/?user_field_names=true' -H 'Authorization: Token [BASEROW_TOKEN]' | python -c "import sys,json; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin)['results']))")
   - If rate limited, note that the next run will retry

Keep it brief — David wants a quick status, not a novel.

jobs.name: AgentMail Reply Monitor
jobs.prompt:
Run: python3 /root/Geeves/scripts/agentmail_replies_to_slack.py

If output contains "No new replies", stop. Do not send any message anywhere.

If output contains "Posted reply" or "Processed", use send_message tool with target "slack" to post the reply details to the home channel.

jobs.name: Google Contacts to Baserow Sync
jobs.prompt:
Run the Google Contacts to Baserow sync to keep contacts in sync.

Steps:
1. Run: cd /root/Geeves/scripts && python3 google_contacts_sync.py --direction google-to-baserow
2. Report the results: how many created, updated, unchanged, errors.
3. If there are errors, log them but don't fail silently.

This is a background maintenance task. David doesn't need to see the details unless something goes wrong.

jobs.name: Mac Mini M4 Refurb Scanner UK
jobs.prompt:
Run the Mac Mini M4 refurb scanner for UK Apple Store.

Step 1: For each of these 3 product pages, use the mcp_firecrawl_firecrawl_scrape tool with formats=["markdown"], onlyMainContent=true, waitFor=8000 to get the JS-rendered page content:

1. https://www.apple.com/uk/shop/product/fcyt4b/a/Refurbished-Mac-mini-Apple-M4-Chip-with-10-Core-CPU-and-10-Core-GPU-Gigabit-Ethernet-
2. https://www.apple.com/uk/shop/product/g1cg1b/a/Refurbished-Mac-mini-Apple-M4-Chip-with-10-Core-CPU-and-10-Core-GPU-Gigabit-Ethernet-
3. https://www.apple.com/uk/shop/product/fcx44b/a/Refurbished-Mac-mini-Apple-M4-Pro-Chip-with-12-Core-CPU-and-16-Core-GPU-Gigabit-Ethernet-

For each page, extract from the markdown content:
- Look for "Delivery:" followed by "- Out of stock" or similar delivery/shipping info
- If "Out of stock" appears → NOT available
- If it shows a delivery estimate like "Delivers in X days" or "Ships in X days" → AVAILABLE
- If neither (no delivery section at all, and "Add to Bag" is present) → likely AVAILABLE
- Extract price, RAM, storage from the content

Step 2: Update the state file at ~/.hermes/mac_mini_refurb_state.json with current availability.

Step 3: Append scan results to ~/.hermes/mac_mini_refurb_scan_log.jsonl as JSON.

Step 4: Determine which configs are AVAILABLE (in stock, deliverable, 24GB+ RAM).

Step 5: ONLY send a Slack message if at least one config is AVAILABLE. Format:
"🍎 Mac Mini M4 Refurb Available (UK):
• M4 24GB/512GB — £849 — Delivers in X days
  https://www.apple.com/uk/shop/product/fcyt4b/a/..."
(Only list configs that are actually available with delivery info)

If NOTHING is available: do NOT send a message. Save results silently.

State file format: {"product_id": {"in_stock": bool, "last_checked": "ISO timestamp"}}
Log file format: one JSON line per scan with timestamp and results.
